hindiTranslation: जो दान बिना सत्कारके तथा अवज्ञापूर्वक अयोग्य देश और कालमें कुपात्रको दिया जाता है, वह दान तामस कहा गया है ॥
englishTranslation: Charity given at the wrong place and time, to an unworthy recipient, without respect and with contempt, is called tamasic.
essence: A gift can become degrading when respect is missing.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A gift given at the wrong time and place, to the wrong person, without respect, is a low form of giving.
simpleInsight: Even generosity loses its value when it is careless or contemptuous.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is classifying kinds of charity for Arjuna on the battlefield, where every action reveals the state of the heart. After praise of pure and self-interested giving, he now names the lowest form: giving badly, to the wrong recipient, with disrespect.
modernRelevance: You hand someone cash while looking at your phone, hoping they go away. The act may look generous, but the attitude turns it cold.
translationLiteral: अदेशकाले (at an improper place and time) / यत् (which) / दानम् (gift / charity) / अपात्रेभ्यः (to unworthy recipients) / च (and) / दीयते (is given) / असत्कृतम् (without respect) / अवज्ञातम् (with contempt) / तत् (that) / तामसम् (tamasic) / उदाहृतम् (is said to be)
insight: It shifts attention from the outer act of giving to the quality of respect behind it.

quoteVariants.text: Even giving can be done with contempt.
Give with respect, or do not give.
